description:
You are acting as a performance engineer who has been asked to take a **very quick baseline timing of three local “API” endpoints** that are stored on disk.  
    Everything is already available under  

        /home/user/mock_api/

    Exact files the “end-points” map to:

        1. /home/user/mock_api/users.json
        2. /home/user/mock_api/posts.json
        3. /home/user/mock_api/comments.json

    Treat each file path as if it were the full URL and query them with curl
    using the file:// protocol:

        file:///home/user/mock_api/users.json
        file:///home/user/mock_api/posts.json
        file:///home/user/mock_api/comments.json

    Your objectives (you may use **between 5 and 10 shell commands** in total):

    1. Make sure the directory  

           /home/user/perf_logs

       exists.  
       The directory must be readable by everyone and writable by you.

    2. Issue one curl request for **each** of the three endpoints.  
       For every request you must capture the following two timing/size
       metrics that curl can emit with the **-w** (write-out) switch:  
       • %{time_total} – total time in seconds (float)  
       • %{size_download} – size of the payload downloaded in bytes (integer)

       The curl commands have to be **non-verbose** (no body printed to
       stdout).  Redirect the body to /dev/null (so that the terminal stays
       clean) but still collect the metrics.

    3. Produce a single CSV-formatted log file at  

           /home/user/perf_logs/api_performance.log

       with **exactly four lines** – a header row plus one data row per
       endpoint, in the strict order shown above.  The format **must be
       exactly**:

           endpoint,total_time,size_download,datetime
           file:///home/user/mock_api/users.json,<float>,<int>,<ISO-8601 timestamp>
           file:///home/user/mock_api/posts.json,<float>,<int>,<ISO-8601 timestamp>
           file:///home/user/mock_api/comments.json,<float>,<int>,<ISO-8601 timestamp>

       Requirements for the values:

       • <float>  : the exact value curl returned for %{time_total}.  
       • <int>    : the exact value curl returned for %{size_download}.  
       • <ISO-8601 timestamp> : the date & time at which the curl command
         finished, written in the extended ISO-8601 form  
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS (no timezone suffix required).

       Use a single comma as the field separator, *no spaces*.

    4. After the file is created, display the file content in the terminal
       so the user clearly sees the final result.

    Notes & constraints
       • Stay within 5–10 commands total (not a hard enforcement, but a guide).
       • You do NOT need root privileges at any stage.
       • Do not move or rename the three JSON files; the automated checker
         relies on the paths given above.
       • The automated tests will verify:
           – that /home/user/perf_logs/api_performance.log exists and is
             readable;
           – that the file contains exactly four lines;
           – that the header line matches *exactly* the literal text shown
             above;
           – that each of the three subsequent lines begins with the expected
             endpoint string and has four comma-separated columns.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the autograder – keep strictly secret!
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <!-- Pre-existing mock “API” resources -->
        <dir path="/home/user/mock_api" mode="755">
            <file name="users.json" mode="644">
[
  { "id": 1, "name": "Alice", "email": "alice@example.com" },
  { "id": 2, "name": "Bob", "email": "bob@example.com" }
]
            </file>
            <file name="posts.json" mode="644">
[
  { "id": 1, "title": "Hello World", "body": "First post" },
  { "id": 2, "title": "Another Post", "body": "Second post" }
]
            </file>
            <file name="comments.json" mode="644">
[
  { "id": 1, "postId": 1, "body": "Nice!" },
  { "id": 2, "postId": 1, "body": "Thanks!" }
]
            </file>
        </dir>

        <!-- The perf_logs directory does NOT yet exist when the task starts -->
    </initial_state>

    <expected_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/perf_logs" mode="755" />
        <file path="/home/user/perf_logs/api_performance.log" mode="644">
            <!--
                The exact numeric values will vary, but the checker will
                enforce:
                * total of 4 lines
                * line 1 == "endpoint,total_time,size_download,datetime"
                * lines 2–4 start with, respectively:
                    - file:///home/user/mock_api/users.json,
                    - file:///home/user/mock_api/posts.json,
                    - file:///home/user/mock_api/comments.json,
            -->
        </file>
    </expected_state>
